(**TLT**) is a transportation company owned by the city of [Tallinn](/wiki/Tallinn "Tallinn"), [Estonia](/wiki/Estonia "Estonia"). TLT is a result of the merger of Tallinn Bus Company (*Tallinna Autobussikoondis*) and Tallinn Tram and Trolleybus Company (*Tallinna Trammi- ja Trollibussikoondis*) in July 2012. The company provides bus, trolleybus, and tram services in Tallinn.

Filippov, Madis (19 July 2012). ["Tallinna Autobussikoondis ja Trammi- ja Trollibussikoondis ühinesid"](http://tallinncity.postimees.ee/912122/tallinna-autobussikoondis-ja-trammi-ja-trollibussikoondis-uhinesid). *Tallinn City* (in Estonian). [Postimees](/wiki/Postimees "Postimees"). Retrieved 1 April 2015.

**Tallinna Linnatranspordi AS** (**TLT**) is a transportation company owned by the city of [Tallinn](/wiki/Tallinn "Tallinn"), [Estonia](/wiki/Estonia "Estonia"). TLT is a result of the merger of Tallinn Bus Company (*Tallinna Autobussikoondis*) and Tallinn Tram and Trolleybus Company (*Tallinna Trammi- ja Trollibussikoondis*) in July 2012. The company provides bus, trolleybus, and tram services in Tallinn.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Tallinna_Linnatranspordi_AS&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/87/197TAK_in_Tallinn.JPG/250px-197TAK_in_Tallinn.JPG)](/wiki/File:197TAK_in_Tallinn.JPG)

[Scania L94UB](/wiki/Scania_4-series_(bus) "Scania 4-series (bus)") in Tallinn in July 2008

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/1d/Tallin_bus_1.jpg/250px-Tallin_bus_1.jpg)](/wiki/File:Tallin_bus_1.jpg)

[Scania CL94UA](/wiki/Scania_4-series_(bus) "Scania 4-series (bus)") in Tallinn in August 2006

### Tallinn Bus Company

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Tallinna_Linnatranspordi_AS&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Tallinn Bus Company")]

TAK was founded in 1945 as a state-owned company, under which status it operated until 1993, when it was reorganised as a public limited company owned by the [City of Tallinn](/wiki/Tallinn "Tallinn"). Currently, the company is divided into seven operating divisions.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

### Tallinn Tram and Trolleybus Company

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Tallinna_Linnatranspordi_AS&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Tallinn Tram and Trolleybus Company")]

Tallinn Tram and Trolleybus Company was started in 1997 and provides transportation ever since.

### Merger

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Tallinna_Linnatranspordi_AS&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Merger")]

Tallinn Tram and Trolleybus Company was merged with Tallinn Bus Company, and Tallinna Linnatranspordi AS officially started its operations under the new name on 18 July 2012.[[1]](#cite_note-1)

At the end of 2019, the company's bus fleet has 529 buses serving 75 bus routes. TLT plans to replace all diesel buses with gas buses by 2025.[[2]](#cite_note-2)
